title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: The (0) ARRIVAL (ARRIVE) of steel and glass changed the way we build our cities. Contemporary architects now focus on (17) SUSTAINABILITY (SUSTAIN) and the efficient use of energy. Many (18) RESIDENTIAL (RESIDENT) buildings now incorporate vertical gardens to improve air quality. This (19) INNOVATIVE (INNOVATE) approach helps to reduce the urban heat island effect. Furthermore, the (20) INSTALLATION (INSTALL) of solar panels is becoming a standard practice. Despite the (21) CRITICISM (CRITIC) from traditionalists who prefer stone structures, modern designs offer more (22) FLEXIBILITY (FLEXIBLE) for growing populations. The (23) REDEVELOPMENT (DEVELOP) of old industrial areas has breathed new life into many downtown districts. These (24) TRANSFORMATIONS (TRANSFORM) prove that cities can evolve while remaining functional and beautiful. By blending technology with environmental awareness, architects are creating spaces that reflect the needs of the future. The result is a skyline that is both visually stunning and responsible, marking a new era for the construction industry and urban living.

example.text: The (0) ARRIVAL (ARRIVE) of steel and glass...
example.explanation: Noun required after the definite article; ARRIVE → ARRIVAL.

questions.explanation: Noun required after the preposition 'on'; SUSTAIN → SUSTAINABILITY.
Adjective required to modify 'buildings'; RESIDENT → RESIDENTIAL.
Noun required after the quantifier 'more'; FLEXIBLE → FLEXIBILITY.
Noun with a prefix indicating 'again' required; DEVELOP → REDEVELOPMENT.
Plural noun required as the subject; TRANSFORM → TRANSFORMATIONS.
